prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a rast has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the rast-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are 5 objects on a table: a rast, a drog, a grup, a mek, and a twix. The rast is underneath the drog. The drog is to-the-left-of the grup. The grup is above the mek. The mek is to-the-right-of the twix. The rast has a yellow button on top, a green button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The drog has a green button on top and a yellow button on side. The grup has a green button on top and a yellow button on front. The mek has a blue button on top and a yellow button on back. The twix has a blue button on top and a yellow button on front. Pressing the yellow button on the rast activates it.

Perception scenario: There are 5 objects on a shelf: a pell, a prit, a woz, a scob, and a ikk. The woz is underneath the scob. The scob is to-the-left-of the ikk. The ikk is above the pell. The pell is to-the-right-of the prit. The pell has a blue button on top and a blue button on bottom. The prit has a green button on top and a blue button on bottom. The woz has a yellow button on top, a green button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The scob has a green button on top, a yellow button on side, and a blue button on bottom. The ikk has a blue button on top and a green button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the rast-analog, and which button activates it?
